author: @RehdBlob

text:
Hey did Mario Paint Composer get an update at some point since I used to be into it?  

I don't recognize those shadow notes.  I'm guessing that's some kind of staccato feature, right? 

Either way, I don't remember anything like that before.  Has it been updated with anything new the past few years or so?  Or did I just never notice it until now?  


Sweet arrangement, by the way.
author: @AngryNerdBird

text: +Goatmon I'm working on making this thing run on more computers but unfortunately I'm at the mercy of what's going on in real life as always. It's moving towards a full release but there are some serious stability issues that need to be addressed first.
author: @RehdBlob

text:
+MediaMunkee Yup, that was the intention! 
:)
author: @RehdBlob

text: +Goatmon Thanks, and I seem to be getting this question a lot so I added an annotation. It's actually a different offshoot composer that's stalled in a bit of an unstable prerelease state but still has several new functions including the staccato thing. https://github.com/DC37/Super-Mario-Paint
author: @MediaMunkee

text:
MediaMunkee Huh. I can't even figure out how to open that thing. lol

Thanks anyway!
author: @AngryNerdBird

text: +Goatmon https://github.com/DC37/Super-Mario-Paint/releases Get the zip from here if you haven't, extract, and run the SMP.bat. Needs Java installed.
author: @MediaMunkee

text: RehdBlob You made a good composer though man! Thanks for that.

text: what are those gray notes?
author: @ahbeef

text:
+ahbeef It's something that afaik only Super Mario Paint has; manual note/instrument cutoffs. Definitely much more convenient than exploiting the low-note cutoff glitch in Mario Paint Composer. SMP also has a per-instrument toggle so that a new note from the same instrument doesn't automatically silence the previous one; very handy if you want a specific sort of reverb effect, or to to use one instrument for two separate tracks. I need to play around with that more.


SMP does have a tendency to crash a lot though.
author: @MediaMunkee

text: oh ok, i might check that out then. it sounds pretty great
author: @ahbeef

text:
+ahbeef 
Advanced Mario Sequencer has per-instrument cutoffs but I took it a little further and made per-note cutoffs on Super Mario Paint.

text: @Gimurei  But it wasn't. It just used the soundfont.
author: @R3SerialDreams2

text: +Gabriel Pelayo I'm pretty sure this whole fight theme uses an RP2A03 chipset soundfont, the reason that sounds so close is that the gameboys in MPC use a basic squarewave like you would hear from that. But he does use the mario paint soundfont in whatever DAW he uses. So he doesn't use MPC but he uses sounds from it. Because even though dogsong is made with only MPC sounds, it would be impossible to recreate it exactly in MPC due to how high and low some of the barks are played. Hope that helps.
author: @JawnGG
